2603.20020.title: Detached Skip-Links and $R$-Probe: Decoupling Feature Aggregation from Gradient Propagation for MLLM OCR
2603.20020.paper_summary: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) excel at high-level reasoning yet fail on OCR tasks where fine-grained visual details are compromised or misaligned. We identify an overlooked optimization issue in multi-layer feature fusion. Skip pathways introduce direct back-propagation paths from high-level semantic objectives to early visual layers. This mechanism overwrites low-level signals and destabilizes training. To mitigate this gradient interference, we propose Detached Skip-Links, a minimal modification that reuses shallow features in the forward pass while stopping gradients through the skip branch during joint training. This asymmetric design reduces gradient interference, improving stability and convergence without adding learnable parameters. To diagnose whether fine-grained information is preserved and usable by an LLM, we introduce $R$-Probe, which measures pixel-level reconstructability of projected visual tokens using a shallow decoder initialized from the first quarter of the LLM layers. Across multiple ViT backbones and multimodal benchmarks, and at scales up to 7M training samples, our approach consistently improves OCR-centric benchmarks and delivers clear gains on general multimodal tasks.
2603.20020.paper_summary_zh: 多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）在高層次推理方面表現出色，但在 OCR 任務中，當細緻的視覺細節受到損害或錯位時，則表現不佳。我們識別出多層特徵融合中被忽視的優化問題。跳過路徑引入了從高層語義目標到早期視覺層的直接反向傳播路徑。這一機制覆蓋了低層信號並使訓練不穩定。為了減輕這種梯度干擾，我們提出了 Detached Skip-Links，這是一種最小的修改，在前向傳遞中重用淺層特徵，同時在聯合訓練期間停止通過跳過分支的梯度。這種不對稱設計減少了梯度干擾，提高了穩定性和收斂性，而不增加可學習的參數。為了診斷細緻信息是否被 LLM 保留和可用，我們引入了 $R$-Probe，它測量使用從 LLM 層的前四分之一初始化的淺層解碼器對投影視覺標記的像素級重建能力。在多個 ViT 骨幹和多模態基準測試中，並在高達 7M 的訓練樣本規模下，我們的方法始終改善了以 OCR 為中心的基準測試，並在一般多模態任務上帶來明顯的增益。
2603.20020.author: Ziye Yuan et.al.
2603.20020.authors: Ziye Yuan, Ruchang Yao, Chengxin Zheng, Yusheng Zhao, Daxiang Dong, Ming Zhang
